title: `Py_DECREF(NULL)` crash in inplace operator error path when `__object_proxy__` lookup fails
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

All 13 inplace operator implementations on the C `ObjectProxy` type contain a bug that causes a guaranteed `Py_DECREF(NULL)` crash (and a leak of an intermediate result object) when `PyObject_GetAttrString(self, "__object_proxy__")` fails.

This was introduced in commit 8b19c0a (October 2025) and affects every inplace operator: `+=`, `-=`, `*=`, `%=`, `**=`, `<<=`, `>>=`, `&=`, `^=`, `|=`, `//=`, `/=`, `@=`.

## Reproducer

```python
import wrapt

class BrokenObjectProxy(wrapt.ObjectProxy):
    @property
    def __object_proxy__(self):
        raise RuntimeError("boom")

p = BrokenObjectProxy(10)
p += 5   # crashes the interpreter (Py_DECREF on NULL)
```

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
